message:
fuse E2E: soft-skip MOVE_EXT tests when kernel/FUSE times out pre-dispatch

The mounted-path MOVE_EXT tests added to fuse_e2e.rs in the previous
commit can hang when the kernel/FUSE stack accepts the ioctl but the
underlying userspace dispatcher — for reasons outside ffs-fuse's
control (FUSE protocol version mismatch, stuck mount, etc.) — never
returns control to the caller. On CI runners where we do not want a
stall to masquerade as failure, this commit adds a 15-second SIGALRM
watchdog inside the embedded Python helper and an explicit skip path
in the Rust test bodies.

Python-side (ext4_move_ext_ioctl script):
- Import signal and install a SIGALRM handler that raises TimeoutError.
- arm signal.alarm(15) before fcntl.ioctl, disarm it in a finally.
- If the ioctl times out (TimeoutError | InterruptedError), emit a JSON
  report `{"timeout": true, "message": "..."}` and exit 0 so the Rust
  side sees a structured skip signal rather than a hung subprocess.
- Preserve the existing OSError errno path; only add the new timeout
  path on top.

Rust-side (fuse_ioctl_ext4_move_ext_swaps_middle_extent_on_mounted_path
and fuse_ioctl_ext4_move_ext_rejects_hole_backed_range_on_mounted_path):
- Read the ioctl trace before branching so timeouts can still cross-
  check that the stall happened *outside* our userspace dispatcher.
- When report["timeout"] == true, assert the ioctl trace did NOT
  contain EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT_CMD — if it did, the timeout happened
  inside ffs-fuse and the test must fail rather than skip.
- On a valid pre-dispatch timeout, emit a stderr skip message and
  return, so the test is green but the incident is visible in
  CI logs.

This preserves the positive/negative assertions already in place
(middle-extent swap success, hole-backed rejection) for all kernels
where the ioctl reaches userspace at all — it only adds a guard
against environment failures that would otherwise stall the run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
